transport_model.description: How the MCP server connects to the harness.

auth_model.description: How authentication is supplied, if any.

secrets_model.description: How secrets are referenced and redacted.

sandbox_model.description: What runtime and filesystem boundary contains the MCP server.

smoke_fixture.description: A safe non-mutating command/tool call that proves startup and basic behavior.

skill_replacement_fit.description: Whether static behavior should move to a deterministic skill instead of MCP.

classification_rules: Use MCP only for live external state, authenticated SaaS, browser/runtime state, telemetry streams, current docs/search, or persistent query services.
Prefer skills for deterministic static transformations, docs generation, local file analysis, and CLI-backed workflows.
Every credentialed MCP must use env references or redacted fingerprints in generated output.
Every remote MCP must document transport, auth, timeout, retry, and failure behavior before validation.
